message: Parameter tuning has stalled. The Gemini agents have been unable to improve the composite score for 3 iterations.

checks.name: temperature_range
checks.reason: Temperatures must stay within physically realizable ocean range

checks.name: equator_pole_gradient
checks.reason: Meridional temperature gradient must exist and be reasonable

checks.name: hemispheric_symmetry
checks.reason: Ocean temperatures are roughly symmetric; large asymmetry suggests broken forcing

checks.name: deep_colder_than_surface
checks.value: 1/15 bands have deep warmer than surface
checks.reason: Deep ocean is always colder than surface (stable stratification)

checks.name: western_intensification
checks.value: Gulf Stream max: 0.912, NA interior max: 1.071, ratio: 0.9x
checks.reason: Stommel: beta effect concentrates return flow on western boundary

checks.name: subtropical_gyre_exists
checks.value: NA subtrop psi: mean=-0.0311, range=0.0405, cells=1173
checks.reason: Wind-driven subtropical gyre from Sverdrup balance

checks.name: acc_eastward_flow
checks.value: Mean zonal flow at 58°S: -0.1486 (360 ocean cells)
checks.reason: Drake Passage open → wind drives eastward ACC

checks.name: deep_water_formation
checks.value: Polar deep: 2.7°C (84 cells), Tropical deep: 4.3°C (278 cells)
checks.reason: Thermohaline: cold dense water sinks at poles, fills deep basins

checks.name: poleward_heat_transport
checks.reason: Ocean must transport heat from tropics to poles — fundamental energy balance

checks.name: atlantic_warmer_than_pacific
checks.reason: Gulf Stream and AMOC transport extra heat into North Atlantic

recentHistory.hypothesis: Failure of western boundary current intensification and excessive poleward heat transport in the mid-latitudes
Failure of western boundary current intensification and excessive poleward heat transport leading to polar temperature inversion.

physicistLastHypothesis.dominant_error: Failure of western boundary current intensification and excessive poleward heat transport leading to polar temperature inversion.

hypotheses.mechanism: The lateral viscosity (A_visc) is too high, effectively 'smearing' the western boundary currents and preventing the formation of tight, high-velocity jets. This leads to inefficient heat transport at the boundaries and forces the model to rely on excessive eddy diffusion, which causes the observed poleward heat transport errors.
hypotheses.side_effects: Reduced stability of the numerical solver; potential for checkerboard instabilities or grid-scale noise in the velocity field.

hypotheses.mechanism: The coupling between the surface and deep layers (gamma_mix) is insufficient to transport heat downward in the tropics, while the deep water formation (gamma_deep_form) is too weak to effectively flush the deep basins with cold polar water, resulting in the deep layer being warmer than the surface in some regions.
hypotheses.side_effects: Significant alteration of the AMOC strength and potential cooling of the global mean surface temperature due to increased vertical heat exchange.

hypotheses.mechanism: The bottom friction (r_friction) is too low to properly constrain the barotropic flow, allowing the interior ocean to develop unrealistic circulation patterns that interfere with the formation of the western boundary currents.
hypotheses.side_effects: Overall reduction in kinetic energy and slowdown of the global circulation, potentially weakening the ACC.

physicistLastHypothesis.recommended: H1 — The visual evidence shows weak western boundary currents (lack of bright, tight filaments in the speed map), which is the primary structural failure causing the zonal temperature errors.

suggestion: Consider whether structural changes to the physics code (index.html) are needed — new forcing terms, different boundary conditions, modified advection scheme — rather than just parameter tuning.
